title: rubus fruticosus subsp. pallidus
intro: Rubus fruticosus subsp. pallidus, also known as wild red raspberry or pale bramble, is a subspecies of the blackberry plant that belongs to the rose family. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, which have been used to treat various health conditions due to their high levels of antioxidants, tannins, and flavonoids. The health benefits of Rubus fruticosus subsp. pallidus include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and aiding in digestion, among others. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
